source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Gas Release on FPSO Turritella

context.region: Gulf of Mexico - Walker Ridge Block 551

actions_taken: Automatic shutdown and blowdown of production system
Gas detection system triggered process shutdown
Engineer closed several valves to stop escaping gas
Engineer received medical treatment and tetanus shot
BSEE issued Incident of Non-Compliance (INC G-110 W)
event.summary: On 26-NOV-2016 at approximately 1406 hours, a high pressure gas release occurred around the gas export metering skid on the FPSO Turritella at Walker Ridge Block 551-A. During an attempt to collect gas samples, an engineer mistakenly pulled a half-inch plug from a double block and bleed valve without first bleeding down the line, despite system pressure of up to 2500 psi. The escaping gas was detected by the gas detection system, triggering an automatic facility shutdown. The engineer sustained a minor injury and received onboard medical treatment. No evacuation was necessary.

threats.name: Failure to depressurize line before plug removal
Miscommunication regarding sample point location and procedure
Lack of stop work authority implementation when worker expressed discomfort

controls.name: Gas detection system
controls.lod_basis: Automated detection system that triggers process shutdown

supporting_text: The escaping gas was detected by the gas detection system, causing a process shutdown.
Alarms sounded, and personnel went to their assigned muster stations.

controls.name: Double block and bleed procedure
controls.barrier_role: Isolation of pressurized line before maintenance
controls.lod_basis: Standard procedure for safe depressurization before component removal

supporting_text: The engineer mistakenly pulled a half-inch plug from the side of the valve without bleeding down the line.
The plug was not isolated from the system pressure, and once the plug was removed, the gas escape occurred.
Subject attempted to take a gas sample, in doing so failed to double block and bleed a Gas Export line resulting in a gas release.

controls.name: Stop work authority
controls.barrier_role: Halt unsafe work when worker expresses discomfort
controls.lod_basis: Safety management procedure to prevent continuation of unsafe activities

supporting_text: Upon realizing that the sample point had been seal-locked by the pipeline company, the lab technician felt uncomfortable with the operation.
When the lab technician stated he felt uncomfortable with taking the gas sample, stop work authority should have been implemented, and the production superintendant should have been notified.

controls.name: Clear work instructions and communication
controls.barrier_role: Ensure workers understand assigned tasks and procedures
controls.lod_basis: Management communication to prevent misunderstanding of work scope

supporting_text: It appears from information gathered during the investigation that either miscommunication or a lack of understanding of the instructions given to the workers by the production superintendant may have been a contributing factor in the chain of events that led to the incident.

controls.name: Pressure indication and awareness
controls.barrier_role: Visual awareness of system pressure before component removal
controls.lod_basis: Pressure gauges visible to personnel performing work

supporting_text: Both employees saw that there were up to 2500 pounds per square inch of pressure on the system in that area at the time.

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
